channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Governadora do Distrito Federal pede ajuda ao governo Lula para crise no BRB
item.description: The governor of the Federal District, Celina Leão (PP), requested assistance from Lula's government to address the financial crisis of BRB (Bank of Brasília), in a phone call with Finance Minister Dario Durigan last Monday (30). The request reflects concerns over the stability of the DF's public financial institution.

item.title: Os múltiplos papéis de Alckmin nas eleições
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin has been confirmed on Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's 2026 ticket, strengthening outreach to centrist voters and recognizing his performance in office, including reversing U.S. tariffs on Brazilian goods. He will also assist Fernando Haddad in his São Paulo gubernatorial campaign, leveraging his strong presence in the state's interior. Haddad showed strong early polling after announcing his run, raising hopes among PT supporters of defeating incumbent governor Tarcísio de Freitas.

item.title: Após meses de espera, Lula oficializa indicação de Jorge Messias ao STF
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva formally nominated Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court (STF), following months of delay after Justice Luís Roberto Barroso's retirement. Messias, the Union's Attorney General and an evangelical Baptist, must undergo Senate confirmation, a process currently hindered by Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who initially opposed the nomination and attempted to rush the hearing. If confirmed, Messias will join the STF's Second Chamber, representing the first Supreme Court nomination since the 19th century not to face senatorial rejection.

item.title: Ciro Gomes se alia a Bolsonaro contra Lula e PT
item.description: Ciro Gomes, former presidential candidate and PSDB leader, is running again in Ceará, possibly for governor, in alliance with Flávio Bolsonaro. Despite his left-wing background, he has become a vehement anti-PT figure due to perceived betrayals by Lula, especially after being sidelined in 2018. This strategic alliance aims to defeat Lula in Ceará, where the PT is strong, leveraging the PSDB's rightward shift and the migration of its electorate to Bolsonaro.

item.title: Competência não tem gênero, diz 1ª mulher general do Brasil
item.description: Medical Colonel Cláudia Lima Gusmão Cacho became the first woman to reach the rank of Army General in Brazilian Army history, assuming command of the Brasília Area Military Hospital. During the ceremony, Defense Minister José Múcio announced an R$ 800 billion investment plan for the Armed Forces, aiming to raise defense spending from 1% to 2-3% of GDP. The event also highlighted progress in gender integration, including women’s inclusion in Communications and combat roles, and the launch of the voluntary Female Military Service with 1,465 pioneers.

item.title: Senado confirma recebimento de mensagem presidencial com indicação de Messias para vaga no STF
item.description: The Senate confirmed receipt of the presidential message nominating Jorge Messias to the STF, after over four months of delay due to bureaucracy and political disagreements with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre. The nomination will now be read in plenary and sent to the CCJ, where Alcolumbre, as committee president, will appoint a rapporteur and set the date for the confirmation hearing. Although Messias has met with around 70 senators to secure the 41 votes needed for approval, the government and Senate remain without agreement on a timeline, and the hearing may be postponed to the second half of the year.

item.title: Lula diz que Ciro Gomes é ‘destemperado’ e acha que ‘sabe tudo’
item.description: Lula criticized Ciro Gomes, calling him 'hot-headed' and saying he 'thinks he knows everything', while also noting his frequent party changes. Despite the criticism, the president acknowledged Ciro's past as a minister and said they took different paths due to Ciro's presidential ambitions. The remarks came amid the 2026 Ceará gubernatorial race, where Ciro leads in polls against PT candidates Elmano de Freitas and Camilo Santana.

item.title: Nova lei expande oferta de creches e pré-escolas em zonas rurais
item.description: Law 15.369/2026 expands municipal obligations to provide daycare and preschool spots in rural areas proportionally to population, reducing urban-rural inequality in early childhood education. The measure corrects a worsening access disparity that reached 19.6 percentage points in 2022, according to PNE data cited by Senator Professora Dorinha Seabra.

item.title: Parcelamento de reajuste no querosene de aviação funcionará como 'empréstimo'; entenda
item.description: Petrobras will introduce an adherence term to mitigate the impact of the aviation kerosene (QAV) price increase, allowing distributors to pay only an 18% increase in April (vs. the contracted 54.8%), with the difference payable in six installments starting July 2026. The measure, aligned with President Lula’s concerns about Middle East conflict effects on global oil prices, aims to preserve demand and ensure the benefit is passed fully to airlines, not retained as corporate profit. Although Petrobras will offset future losses to protect shareholders, officials stress the need for price monitoring at pumps and distributors to prevent the measure from being undermined by downstream price hikes.

item.title: PGR recorre de decisão do STF que anulou condenação de Garotinho
item.description: The Attorney General’s Office appealed the decision by Supreme Court Justice Cristiano Zanin that annulled Anthony Garotinho’s conviction in the ‘Chequinho’ operation, arguing Zanin contradicted a unanimous 2022 ruling by the Court’s 2nd Chamber. The PGR maintains other evidence supports the conviction and requests Zanin reconsider or that the 1st Chamber overturn the habeas corpus, noting the decision was made without hearing the TSE or the Prosecution.

item.title: Diesel será mais caro em Estados fora de acordo, diz ministro
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan said diesel will be more expensive in states that do not join the federal and state subsidy proposal of R$1.20 per liter, split equally between the Union and states. He noted that only 'very few' states have not yet adhered to the measure, which will be published as a Provisional Measure this week after President Lula returns from travel. The goal is to mitigate the impact of the Middle East war on domestic fuel prices.

item.title: “Vamos ter que colocar alguém na cadeia”, diz Lula sobre abusos no diesel
item.description: President Lula stated that someone will have to be jailed if abusive diesel price increases continue, accusing individuals of bad character of being paid not to raise prices but doing so anyway. He said the government is monitoring the situation and will act rigorously against illegal price hikes.

item.title: ‘Destempero’ de Ciro não dá resultado na política, diz Lula
item.description: Lula criticized Ciro Gomes' behavior, describing him as 'temperamental' and stating that his attitude creates unnecessary conflicts and yields no political results, despite maintaining personal respect and denying any rupture. He contrasted his own loyalty to the PT with Ciro's frequent party changes. Despite the criticism, Lula acknowledged Ciro's past role as minister and said he could still contribute to the country if he changed his conduct.

item.title: Entrevista: 'Eleitor de centro vai comparar os governos Lula e Bolsonaro e definir a eleição', diz Fufuca
item.description: Former Minister André Fufuca states that the center voter will decide the 2026 election by comparing Lula and Bolsonaro's governments, emphasizing PT's advantages in employment, inflation, and minimum wage. He criticizes the government's communication failures in the Master case and distances himself from Senator Ciro Nogueira, while warning that an alliance between União-PP and Flávio Bolsonaro could give the opposition a TV time advantage.

item.title: Economista avalia a escolha de Alckmin na chapa de Lula
item.description: Economist Alex Agostini evaluates that Geraldo Alckmin’s permanence on Lula’s ticket is a political necessity of the government, serving as a moderate figure with smooth dialogue in Congress and economic sectors. His departure from the Ministry of Development, Industry, Commerce and Services will have limited impact, as technical staff ensure continuity of ongoing projects. Alckmin remains a strategic advisor behind the scenes, reinforcing market perceptions of stability and predictability.

item.title: Esposa de deputado federal se manifesta após denunciá-lo por agressão. Veja vídeo
item.description: Fabiana Khauam Marangoni, wife of federal deputy Fernando Marangoni (Podemos), confirmed she was assaulted by him on April 1st in Santo André following an argument. She denied claims that the apartment was destroyed but requested he leave the home. Both gave testimony at the Women’s Police Station, where the deputy was taken on suspicion of domestic violence.

item.title: TCU investiga a participação de funcionários públicos no desfile que homenageou Lula na Sapucaí
item.description: The TCU has launched an investigation into whether public officials were improperly involved in the carnival parade honoring President Lula, following a request from the Novo Party. The tribunal asked the Civil House and Secom for details on federal employees who traveled to Rio between February 1 and 18, 2026, to support Janja, Lula, or ministers, including costs and specific tasks like sending invitations and taking measurements for costumes. First Lady Janja withdrew from participating in the 'Friends of Lula' float to avoid controversy, being replaced by Fafá de Belém, and watched the parade alongside Lula and Rio Mayor Eduardo Paes.

item.title: Sancionada criação do Selo de Engenharia ou Arquitetura Solidária
item.description: Law 15.370/2026 establishes the Solidarity Engineering or Architecture Seal, awarded to construction sector professionals and companies that implement or finance projects benefiting low-income, indigenous, and traditional communities, primarily those registered in CadÚnico. The seal features three tiers — beginner, intermediate, and advanced — with criteria to be set by executive regulation, and includes incentives such as tax exemptions, public land donations, and use of public spaces. The law also mandates equitable hiring practices and the adoption of sustainable, universally accessible construction techniques.

item.title: Cláudia Gusmão recebe espada e bastão de comando como primeira general mulher do Brasil; fotos
item.description: Dr. Cláudia Lima Gusmão Cacho became the first woman to attain the rank of general in the Brazilian Army, receiving the general’s sword and command baton in a ceremony at the Army Club in Brasília. Her promotion, approved by the Army’s High Command in February and signed by President Lula, marks a historic milestone in an institution that only began admitting women into permanent ranks in the 1990s. The ceremony also advanced other officers, underscoring military hierarchy amid rising global tensions.

item.title: Liderança de Flávio Bolsonaro em São Paulo mostra que MG será o VAR da eleição presidencial
item.description: The Atlas poll shows Flávio Bolsonaro leading in São Paulo, indicating that Minas Gerais will be the decisive state in the 2026 presidential election. The right seeks to consolidate support in São Paulo, the South, and agro states, while the PT focuses on the Northeast and North, with Minas as the strategic battleground. The entry of new candidates like Ronaldo Caiado and Rodrigo Pacheco may fragment the vote but tends to strengthen existing coalitions.

item.title: Lula diz que Faria Lima deve estar irritada com ele por investir em educação para pobres
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that federal government investments in education and social programs, such as Pé-de-Meia, go against the interests of the financial market and that the economic elite, including Faria Lima, must be irritated with him for prioritizing the poor. He made these remarks during an event in Fortaleza, again criticizing income concentration and the role of the financial sector in Brazilian politics.

item.title: As trocas no TSE com as quais Castro conta para reverter inelegibilidade
item.description: Claudio Castro, former governor of Rio de Janeiro, is seeking to overturn his ineligibility decided by the TSE after being convicted in 2022 for hiring electoral cabos through the Instituto Ceperj. He is counting on two changes in the court: the replacement of Minister Isabel Gallotti (who issued the condemning vote) by Ricardo Cuevas at the STJ, and the possible substitution of Cármen Lúcia (who limited the time for view) by Dias Toffoli at the STF. If both votes are reversed, he would have 4 votes in favor of acquittal against 3, allowing him to run for Senate even if his criminal conviction stands.

item.title: Flávio tem 44,8% e Lula tem 37,5% em disputa pelo Planalto no AP
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) leads the presidential race in Amapá with 44.8% of voting intentions, followed by Lula (PT) with 37.5%, according to an AtlasIntel survey conducted between March 23 and 28, 2026. In a potential runoff scenario, Flávio Bolsonaro defeats Lula with 50.1% to 40.2%. The survey has a 3 percentage point margin of error and a 95% confidence level.

item.title: O ‘sucessor natural’ de Lula e a pesquisa que traz um susto para a campanha do petista
item.description: President Lula continues to view Fernando Haddad as his natural successor, despite exploring alternatives like Camilo Santana, whose appeal is tied to education policy. In Ceará, the gubernatorial race has gained strategic importance, with Ciro Gomes leading in polls, raising concerns at the Planalto due to the region’s symbolic and electoral weight. Regional dynamics in Ceará directly influence national projections, as each vote there affects the balance between Lula’s allies and opponents.

item.title: El Supremo muestra su escepticismo sobre la orden de Trump de denegar la nacionalidad a hijos de inmigrantes no residentes
item.description: A majority of the nine U.S. Supreme Court justices expressed skepticism toward Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to deny birthright citizenship to children of undocumented immigrants and temporary visitors. If enacted, the policy could affect over 250,000 babies annually and nearly three million children over the next decade, raising serious constitutional concerns under the 14th Amendment.

item.title: Guerra ocupa mais espaço na mídia que a paz, diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that war receives more media attention than peace and lies spread more online than truth, during an event in Fortaleza. He emphasized education as an obsession and a key investment for equal opportunity, citing the Pé-de-Meia program and the new ITA campus inauguration. Lula was accompanied by Education Minister Camilo Santana and Ceará Governor Elmano de Freitas.

item.title: Alcolumbre diz que pode segurar análise da indicação de Messias ao STF após mal-estar com Planalto
item.description: Senate President Davi Alcolumbre threatens to delay the analysis of Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF after feeling disrespected by Lula's premature announcement of the nomination without prior consultation. While the government cites bureaucratic delays, not political ones, Alcolumbre accuses the Planalto of political disarray and emphasizes his authority to schedule the vote, amid growing tensions with government leader Jaques Wagner and the departure of key institutional articulators.

item.title: Thriller político “A Conspiração Condor” une Jango, JK e Lacerda
item.description: The political thriller 'A Conspiração Condor', directed by André Sturm, premieres on April 9, 2026, and explores the hypothesis that the deaths of former Brazilian presidents Juscelino Kubitschek and João Goulart, as well as Carlos Lacerda, were the result of a conspiracy involving Brazilian and U.S. government agents, set against the backdrop of Operation Condor. Filmed in Iguape (SP) in 2024 with a budget of R$ 7 million, the film stars Mel Lisboa, Dan Stulbach, and Pedro Bial, blending historical facts with fiction to challenge official narratives surrounding their deaths.

item.title: Lula: ‘Faria Lima deve estar irritada com pobre na escola’
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized the financial market during an event in Fortaleza, defending federal investments in education and social programs, particularly the Pé-de-Meia initiative, which has allocated R$18.6 billion to keep students in high school. He argued these are strategic investments to reduce historical inequalities and ensure equal opportunities, mocking the likely dissatisfaction of São Paulo's 'Faria Lima' financial district with policies benefiting the poor.

item.title: Dr. Furlan venceria no 1º turno disputa ao governo do Amapá
item.description: According to an AtlasIntel poll released on April 1, 2026, former Macapá mayor Dr. Furlan (PSD) would win the first round of the Amapá gubernatorial election with 65.7% voting intention, while incumbent Governor Clécio Luis (União Brasil) trails at 31%. The survey, conducted March 23–28, 2026, among 1,029 voters, has a 95% confidence level and a 3-point margin of error.

item.title: EUA podem esticar corda e voltar à carga sobre o PIX e big techs
item.description: The U.S. is expected to renew pressure on Brazil over Pix and the treatment of American big techs under a Section 301 investigation, seeking leverage in bilateral relations. This pressure could influence the 2026 election, as attacking Pix — widely popular — strengthens Lula’s sovereignty narrative and may benefit the PT campaign. The right, including Flávio Bolsonaro’s circle, has not yet fully recognized the risk, despite his recent polling lead over Lula within the margin of error.

item.title: Planalto não envia ao Senado mensagem da indicação de Messias ao STF 24 horas após anúncio; demora é atribuída à Casa Civil
item.description: Twenty-four hours after the Presidency's Secretariat of Social Communication (Secom) announced that the government would send the presidential message formalizing Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF to the Senate, the document had not yet arrived. The delay is attributed to the Civil House, which cites bureaucratic procedures, while government allies express surprise and claim ignorance of the reason. Messias' nomination, announced in November, has faced Senate resistance, particularly from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who backed another candidate, leading to a rift between the Legislature and the Executive.

item.title: Secretário de Defesa do Consumidor, Gutemberg Fonseca, deixa cargo para disputar eleição
item.description: Rio de Janeiro's interim governor Ricardo Couto removed State Secretary of Consumer Defense Gutemberg de Paula Fonseca from office so he could run for a federal deputy seat in the 2026 elections. Affiliated with the PL party, Fonseca was already considered a pre-candidate and stepped down as part of the government's incompatibility process for senior officials. His successor, Rogério da Costa Pimenta, a former advisor and FGV professor, was appointed upon Fonseca's own recommendation.

item.title: Conselheiro de Trump volta a apoiar Flávio: “Bolsonaro 2026. Let’s Go!”
item.description: Senior Trump adviser Jason Miller publicly endorsed Flávio Bolsonaro’s 2026 presidential bid, posting photos with the senator and Eduardo Bolsonaro on X (formerly Twitter) with the caption 'Bolsonaro 2026. Let’s go!'. The endorsement followed a meeting at CPAC, the premier international conservative forum, highlighting growing ties between the American right and the Bolsonaro family.

item.title: No Ceará, Lula cita investimentos em educação e diz que 'Faria Lima deve estar put*' com ele
item.description: Lula defended investments in education and social assistance as priorities over fiscal austerity, criticating the dissatisfaction of the financial market represented by Avenida Faria Lima. He argued that governing requires choosing between infrastructure projects and ensuring food and education for the poor, highlighting the Pé-de-Meia program with R$18 billion in investment. The president also encouraged youth to enter politics and reaffirmed his commitment to combating violence against women.

item.title: O aumento do número de turistas europeus no Brasil
item.description: Brazil recorded 411,804 European tourist arrivals in the first two months of 2026, an 18.55% increase compared to the same period in 2025, driven by significant growth from Portugal (+29.7%), Germany (+17%), the UK (+14.5%) and France (+8.6%). In 2025, the country welcomed 1.77 million European tourists, a 19.54% rise from 2024, following a 20% increase between 2023 and 2024. Embratur president Marcelo Freixo is stepping down from his post this Wednesday to run for federal deputy from Rio de Janeiro.

item.title: A disputa entre Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro em MG, CE, SC e AP, segundo a AtlasIntel
item.description: AtlasIntel poll reveals a polarized race between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in four Brazilian states. Lula leads comfortably in Ceará, while Flávio Bolsonaro leads in Santa Catarina and Amapá. In Minas Gerais, Lula holds a technical lead, but the race remains within the margin of error, indicating a tight contest.

item.title: EUA mentiram para atacar o Irã, afirma Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva accused the United States of lying about Iran’s nuclear program to justify war against the country, stating the conflict is raising fuel prices and pressuring Brazil’s economy. He referenced the 2010 Tehran Declaration, brokered by Brazil, Turkey, and Iran, which allowed Iran to enrich uranium for peaceful purposes under international monitoring — an agreement rejected by the U.S. and the European Union. Lula said the Brazilian government is implementing fuel tax limits and active monitoring to prevent price abuses and shield vulnerable populations from the war’s economic impact.

item.title: Crusoé: Dr. Furlan lidera corrida ao governo no Amapá, aponta AtlasIntel
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll released on April 1 shows that former Macapá mayor Antônio Furlan, known as Dr. Furlan (PSD), leads the race for governor of Amapá with 65.7% of voting intentions in the first round. Incumbent governor and re-election candidate Clécio Luís (União Brasil) follows with 31%. Other candidates were not reported with significant percentages.

item.title: O desmonte do espólio de Cláudio Castro pelo desembargador Ricardo Couto
item.description: Acting Rio Governor Ricardo Couto has dismissed several allies of Cláudio Castro, including Gutemberg Fonseca, former Consumer Defense Secretary appointed by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, who is expected to run for federal deputy under the PL. Also removed were Jair Bittencourt, former Government Secretary, and Edu Guimarães from the Institutional Security Cabinet, amid a judicial imbroglio extending Couto’s tenure. The moves signal a dismantling of Castro’s political legacy in the Guanabara Palace.

item.title: AP: Pesquisa aponta vitória de Dr. Furlan no 1º turno para o governo
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll released on Wednesday, April 1st, shows Macapá Mayor Dr. Furlan (PSD) would win the Amapá gubernatorial election in the first round with 65.7% of voting intentions, against 31% for incumbent Governor Clécio Luis (União Brasil). In a simulated runoff, Furlan leads 65.4% to 31.7%. The survey highlights Furlan’s strong positive image (75%) and Luis’s high disapproval (59%), with crime, corruption, and healthcare identified as top voter concerns.

item.title: Pesquisa AtlasIntel reforça o favoritismo do casal Furlan nas eleições no Amapá
item.description: The AtlasIntel poll shows Dr. Furlan (PSD) leading the Amapá gubernatorial race with 67.7% in Scenario 1 and 65.4% in Scenario 2, ahead of incumbent governor Clécio Luís (União Brasil). His wife, Rayssa Furlan (Podemos), leads the Senate race with 33.7% to 34.4%, followed by Lucas Barreto (PSD). Dr. Furlan’s recent resignation following a STF investigation did not significantly damage his popularity.

item.title: Lira dá ultimato a prefeito de Maceió e ameaça se aliar a ex-relator da CPI
item.description: Former Chamber of Deputies president Arthur Lira (PP-AL), a pre-candidate for the Senate, issued an ultimatum to Maceio’s mayor JHC to commit to joining his 2026 election ticket. Lira threatened to ally with the former rapporteur of the Public Works CPI if the mayor refuses, signaling a potential breakdown in local political alignment. The move highlights growing tensions within Alagoas’ governing coalition ahead of the state and national elections.

item.title: Eleição direta no RJ pode virar campanha para Flávio Bolsonaro e preocupa aliados de Paes
item.description: Allies of former Rio mayor Eduardo Paes fear that a special direct election for governor, scheduled for June, could become a campaign opportunity for Senator Flávio Bolsonaro to bolster his political standing and support for PL candidate Douglas Ruas, with an eye toward a future presidential run. Despite Paes leading in polls (46% vs. 13% for Ruas), allies worry Flávio must travel across the state to consolidate Bolsonaro family influence, relying on estimated support from 70 of Rio’s 92 mayors. The STF will rule on the legality of the direct election on April 8.

item.title: Sem maioria no Senado, Lula diz que “senador pensa que é Deus”
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that senators, feeling 'like God' due to their eight-year mandates, can create problems for the government if there is no solid base in the Senate. He emphasized the importance of building an allied base in Congress, including allowing ministers to leave office to run for Senate seats, as a central strategy for 2026. Lula also stressed the need to negotiate with allies beyond the PT, citing Camilo Santana as an example of effective governance through political dialogue and alliances.

item.title: STF julga se Jair Bolsonaro podia bloquear cidadãos em contas oficiais nas redes sociais
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) will rule on May 20 on two cases examining whether former President Jair Bolsonaro could block citizens on his official social media accounts (Twitter/X and Instagram) while in office, allegedly violating freedom of expression and access to information. The plaintiffs — journalist William de Luca Martinez and lawyer Leonardo Medeiros Magalhães — argue the blocks constituted censorship and abuse of power, while Bolsonaro’s defense maintained his accounts were private and that he had the right to choose his followers, like any private citizen.

item.title: Fala de Lula sobre senadores irrita líderes e tensiona ambiente para análise de indicação de Messias
item.description: President Lula's remark that senators "think they are God" provoked backlash in Congress, particularly from the opposition, who viewed it as an attempt to subordinate the legislature and a threat to Jorge Messias' STF nomination. While centrist senators downplayed public impact, there are private concerns that the comment undermines government coalition-building and could chill negotiations over the nomination, especially among undecided members of the CCJ.

item.title: Os destaques da pauta de abril e maio no STF: Reforma tributária, eleições no RJ e Ferrogrão
item.description: The STF's agenda for April and May 2026 features high-impact electoral and economic cases, including the decision on whether the Rio de Janeiro interim government election should be direct (popular vote) or indirect (legislative vote), the constitutionality of the law reducing Jamanxim National Park to enable the Ferrogrão railway, and the payroll tax exemption for 17 sectors. Additional topics include tax reform, the appointment of relatives to public offices (already supported by a majority), petroleum royalty distribution rules, and lawsuits on gender pay equity and access to financial intelligence reports without judicial authorization.

item.title: Ibovespa avança com alívio global e otimismo sobre possível trégua no Oriente Médio
item.description: The Ibovespa rose on Wednesday, tracking global optimism over a possible ceasefire in the Middle East following statements by Donald Trump suggesting the conflict could end soon. Major Brazilian banks traded higher, with Banco do Brasil leading gains. Domestically, President Lula attended events in Ceará, including the inauguration of ITA campus works and the two-year anniversary of the Pé-de-Meia program, before traveling to Salvador.

item.title: Nova lei obriga municípios a oferecer vagas em creches e pré-escolas também em áreas rurais
item.description: The new Law 15.369/26 requires municipalities to offer vacancies in daycare centers and preschools in both urban and rural areas, proportionally to each zone's population. The amendment to the National Education Guidelines and Framework Law (LDB) aims to reduce inequality in access to early childhood education between cities and countryside. The measure was signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and originated from Bill 8618/17 by Deputy Damião Feliciano (União-PB).
